trivia_bullets.text: Frantic Inventory is an original card first printed in Core Set 2021 (released July 3, 2020) — not a reprint. It was previewed on June 10, 2020 via Trusted Reviews. Its self-referential draw mechanic (drawing more cards for each copy in the graveyard) places it in the lineage of cards like Accumulated Knowledge and Think Twice that reward running multiples. [Scryfall; Scryfall preview field]

Frantic Inventory's Pauper penny_rank of 600 (Scryfall field) is the highest of this five-card batch — indicating it is one of the most-played cards in budget formats that permit penny-priced cards. It is a confirmed staple in Pauper Izzet Faeries and Izzet Skred archetypes, where running four copies means the fourth Frantic Inventory in a turn draws four cards for {1}{U}. EDHREC rank 28,775 reflects low Commander uptake (it underperforms in singleton formats by design). [Scryfall; cardsrealm.com Izzet Faeries deck list via WebSearch]
sources: cardsrealm.com Izzet Faeries deck list via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: "Another village, another angry mob. Why do I even bother unpacking?" — No speaker attributed. The flavor text establishes the depicted wizard as an itinerant fugitive scholar — someone perpetually expelled and perpetually restarting, hoarding knowledge across repeated exiles. The 'unpacking' gag mirrors the card's mechanic: rummaging through accumulated discards (graveyard copies) for more resources. [Scryfall]

Artist Aaron Miller illustrated Frantic Inventory. Miller is a regular MTG contributor whose work spans multiple sets; his Frantic Inventory composition — cool blue underlighting from below, manic-eyed wizard, cluttered props — leans into comedic character illustration rather than epic fantasy, a register that aligns with the card's joke flavor text. [Scryfall]

Community sentiment (MTGArena Zone card page, Izzet Faeries archetype analysis on cardsrealm.com): Frantic Inventory is considered a sleeper common that reveals its power only in multiples — single-copy draw in Commander is unremarkable, but the Pauper four-of configuration turns it into a lopsided value engine. The card's comedic flavor has made it a minor community favorite for the 'wizard-on-the-run' archetype that shows up throughout blue's flavor history. [MTGArena Zone card page; cardsrealm.com — sentiment]
sources: MTGArena Zone card page via WebSearch snippet

related_cards.name: Accumulated Knowledge
related_cards.note: Functional predecessor: also draws more cards per copy in graveyard; Pauper Izzet lists sometimes run both or choose between them

related_cards.name: Think Twice
related_cards.note: Blue instant card-draw with flashback; shares design space as 'better with multiple copies in graveyard' draw spell

related_cards.name: Mystic Sanctuary
related_cards.note: Island subtype land that recurs instants/sorceries; community notes Frantic Inventory + Mystic Sanctuary as a recurring-draw engine in Pauper

related_cards.name: Brainstorm
related_cards.note: Blue staple card-draw; the reference point against which Frantic Inventory's ceiling and floor are measured in format discussions
